entries.title: Bank of England appoints Rhys Phillips as the new Chief Cashier
entries.description: The Governor of the Bank of England, Andrew Bailey, has announced that Rhys Phillips will be the next Chief Cashier and Director of Notes. He will take up the role on 19 October 2026.

entries.title: Minutes of the London FXJSC Main Committee Meeting – 26 March 2026
entries.description: The Bank of England chairs the London Foreign Exchange Joint Standing Committee (FXJSC), which is a forum for discussion of the wholesale foreign exchange market. The FXJSC is made up of market participants, infrastructure providers and the UK financial regulators.

entries.title: The Retail Payments Infrastructure Board launches consultation on the next generation UK payments infrastructure
entries.description: Today marks a major milestone in the modernisation of the UK's payments landscape, with the Retail Payments Infrastructure Board (RPIB) launching a consultation on the future design of the UK's next-generation retail payments infrastructure.

feeds.name: CBO — Budget & Economic Outlook
feeds.description: Nonpartisan budgetary and economic analysis for the U.S. Congress.

entries.title: Monthly Budget Review: June 2026
entries.description: The federal budget deficit totaled $1.4 trillion in the first nine months of fiscal year 2026, CBO estimates. That amount is $35 billion more than the deficit recorded during the same period last fiscal year.

entries.title: Options to Counter Small Unmanned Aircraft Systems
entries.description: CBO describes current methods to detect and counter small unmanned aircraft systems and estimates it would cost about $74 million to outfit a typical military installation with a layered defense and at least $5 million a year to support it.

entries.title: Estimated Effects of Selected Provisions of H.R. 8870, the BUILD America 250 Act
entries.description: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure on May 22, 2026

entries.title: S. 1868, Critical Access for Veterans Care Act
entries.description: As ordered reported by the Senate Committee on Veterans’ Affairs on March 18, 2026

entries.title: Legislation considered under suspension of the Rules of the House of Representatives during the week of July 13, 2026
entries.description: The Majority Leader of the House of Representatives announces bills that will be considered under suspension of the rules in that chamber. CBO estimates the effects of those bills on direct spending and revenues.

entries.title: S. 933, NASA Authorization Act of 2026
entries.description: As ordered reported by the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation on March 4, 2026

entries.title: S. 3468, National Programmable Cloud Laboratories Network Act of 2025

entries.title: H.R. 3535, Stop Foreign Funds in Elections Act
entries.description: As ordered reported by the House Committee on House Administration on May 14, 2026

entries.title: H.R. 8738, FEC Administrative Improvements Act
entries.description: As ordered reported by the House Committee on House Administration on May 14, 2026

entries.title: Estimating the Economic Effects of Federally Funded R&D: Working Paper 2026-08
entries.description: CBO describes its current analytical framework for modeling the economic effects of changes in federal funding for R&D, focusing on two approaches that CBO has developed: an R&D capital stock approach and an R&D components approach.

entries.title: Assessing the effects of recent provisioning rules on consumer credit allocation in Colombia
entries.description: by Diego Cuesta-Mora, Fredy Gamboa, Camilo Sanchez-Quinto Colombia's post-pandemic recovery in 2021–2022 was marked by rapid consumer credit growth, followed by deteriorating credit quality indicators amid tightening financial conditions. In January 2023, the Superintendence of Finance of Colombia (SFC) introduced higher provisioning requirements for long-term consumer loans to enhance financial resilience against credit risk materialization and to help moderate the rapid expansion of consumer credit observed prior to the reform. From the perspective of credit institutions (CIs), increased provisions imply higher expenses and potential profitability pressures, which could lead to adjustments in lending strategies.

entries.title: Project Insight: uncovering the complexities of global value chains
entries.description: Project Insight developed a proof-of-concept dashboard to enhance policymakers' understanding and monitoring of global supply chain developments. By unifying detailed firm-level datasets, macroeconomic information and advanced analytics within a collaborative platform, the project demonstrated how granular and complex GVC dependencies can be studied more effectively.

entries.title: On the comparison of capital requirements for global systemically important banks
entries.description: Basel III introduced a comprehensive package of capital reforms to bolster the resilience of the global banking system. While the standard supports regulatory convergence across jurisdictions by establishing a common minimum set of requirements for internationally active banks, it also gives national authorities flexibility to exceed the minimum standards. Heterogeneous applications of Basel III across jurisdictions have fuelled perceptions of an uneven playing field and have become central to current debates on how regulation may influence banks' competitiveness.

entries.title: Blockchain consensus mechanisms and fragmentation
entries.description: by Daniel Eidan, Jon Frost, Rudraksh Kansal, Ulf Lewrick, Sang Hyuk Lim, Tomasz Rybarczyk While all permissionless blockchains use token-based incentives to sustain honest validation, differences in how validator rewards, coordination and participation are structured lead to distinct equilibria and trade-offs between decentralisation, security and scalability. These trade-offs underpin the emergence of multiple layer 1 networks and the expansion of layer 2 solutions, resulting in fragmentation of infrastructure, liquidity and assets across and within chains. Tools to mitigate fragmentation – eg bridges and native multi-chain issuance – can reduce frictions, but they reintroduce new dependencies on trust, governance and operational resilience.

entries.title: What makes money, money? Ensuring trust in the next generation financial ecosystem
entries.description: Remarks by Ms Andréa M Maechler, Deputy General Manager, Bank for International Settlements (BIS), at the Point Zero Forum, Zurich, 24 June 2026.

entries.title: Timur M Suleimenov: Statement - base rate of the National Bank of Kazakhstan
entries.description: Statement by Mr Timur M Suleimenov, Governor of the National Bank of Kazakhstan, on the base rate of the National Bank of Kazakhstan, Astana, 5 June 2026.

entries.title: Zhu Hexin: New landscape of global capital flows and China's high-level opening-up
entries.description: Keynote speech by Mr Zhu Hexin, Deputy Governor of the People's Bank of China and SAFE Administrator, at the 2026 Lujiazui Forum, Shanghai, 17 June 2026.

entries.title: Andrew Hauser: "The straight line belongs to man, the curved line belongs to God"
entries.description: Text of the Sir Douglas Copland Memorial Lecture to the Economic Society of Australia (Victoria) by Mr Andrew Hauser, Deputy Governor of the Reserve Bank of Australia, Melbourne, 24 June 2026.

entries.title: Naoki Tamura: Economic activity, prices and monetary policy in Japan
entries.description: Speech by Mr Naoki Tamura, Member of the Policy Board of the Bank of Japan, at a meeting with local leaders, Hyogo, 25 June 2026.

entries.title: Lisa D Cook: Welcome remarks - State of Small Business Symposium
entries.description: Welcome remarks (via pre-recorded video) by Ms Lisa D Cook, Member of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, at the State of Small Business Symposium, hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland, 24 June 2026.

entries.title: Assessing the effects of recent provisioning rules on consumer credit allocation in Colombia
entries.description: by Diego Cuesta-Mora, Fredy Gamboa, Camilo Sanchez-Quinto Colombia's post-pandemic recovery in 2021–2022 was marked by rapid consumer credit growth, followed by deteriorating credit quality indicators amid tightening financial conditions. In January 2023, the Superintendence of Finance of Colombia (SFC) introduced higher provisioning requirements for long-term consumer loans to enhance financial resilience against credit risk materialization and to help moderate the rapid expansion of consumer credit observed prior to the reform. From the perspective of credit institutions (CIs), increased provisions imply higher expenses and potential profitability pressures, which could lead to adjustments in lending strategies.

entries.title: On the comparison of capital requirements for global systemically important banks
entries.description: Basel III introduced a comprehensive package of capital reforms to bolster the resilience of the global banking system. While the standard supports regulatory convergence across jurisdictions by establishing a common minimum set of requirements for internationally active banks, it also gives national authorities flexibility to exceed the minimum standards. Heterogeneous applications of Basel III across jurisdictions have fuelled perceptions of an uneven playing field and have become central to current debates on how regulation may influence banks' competitiveness.

entries.title: Blockchain consensus mechanisms and fragmentation
entries.description: by Daniel Eidan, Jon Frost, Rudraksh Kansal, Ulf Lewrick, Sang Hyuk Lim, Tomasz Rybarczyk While all permissionless blockchains use token-based incentives to sustain honest validation, differences in how validator rewards, coordination and participation are structured lead to distinct equilibria and trade-offs between decentralisation, security and scalability. These trade-offs underpin the emergence of multiple layer 1 networks and the expansion of layer 2 solutions, resulting in fragmentation of infrastructure, liquidity and assets across and within chains. Tools to mitigate fragmentation – eg bridges and native multi-chain issuance – can reduce frictions, but they reintroduce new dependencies on trust, governance and operational resilience.

entries.title: Annual Economic Report 2026
entries.description: This year's Annual Economic Report examines how the global economy is faring as progress meets rising perils – including a new fiscal-financial stability nexus and shifting inflation dynamics.

entries.title: Public debt and monetary policy transmission: evidence from advanced and emerging Europe
entries.description: by Christopher Johns, Aaron Mehrotra, Fabrizio Zampolli Using high-frequency euro area monetary policy shocks and panel local projections for the period 2001-2020, this paper examines how macroeconomic variables respond based on the level and the maturity structure of public debt. The results show that public debt plays a significant role in influencing monetary policy transmission.

entries.title: Financial and real effects of fiscal risk
entries.description: by Denis Gorea, Ding Xuan Ng, Fabrizio Zampolli This paper estimates the macroeconomic and financial effects of fiscal risk shocks using a novel identification from bond yields. We first recover country-specific fiscal risk shocks from a daily Bayesian VAR model in sovereign and safe corporate bond yields, identified via contemporaneous sign restrictions that capture portfolio rebalancing away from government debt toward private safe assets.

entries.title: Annual Report 2025/26
entries.description: The Annual Report introduces the BIS's new strategy and shows how the BIS has supported stakeholders throughout the year.

entries.title: Implementation of global regulatory framework for cryptoassets and stablecoins - Executive Summary
entries.description: The rapid growth of cryptoasset markets and the increasing integration of stablecoins into financial systems have prompted the Financial Stability Board (FSB) to take action. In July 2023, the FSB published its global regulatory framework for cryptoasset activities (FSB Crypto Framework), consisting of high-level recommendations for cryptoasset activities and markets (CA recommendations) and revised recommendations for global stablecoin arrangements (GSC recommendations), based on the principle of "same activity, same risk, same regulation".

entries.title: The macroeconomics of stablecoins
entries.description: by Boris Hofmann, Matthias Kaldorf, Matthias Rottner We analyse the macroeconomic impact of stablecoins using a quantitative macroeconomic model. Stablecoins influence the economy through two opposing channels: (i) a bank lending channel, as household demand for stablecoins raises deposit rates, increases bank funding costs, and reduces loan supply; and (ii) a fiscal space channel, as stablecoin issuers' demand for Treasury bills lowers sovereign borrowing costs, expands fiscal space for tax reductions or higher spending.

entries.title: Stablecoin remuneration on centralised exchanges
entries.description: by Wenqian Huang, Nikola Tarashev, Xinyi Wang Centralised exchanges remunerate stablecoin holders, using the return on the issuer's reserve assets or income from market activity. Under the reserve-based model, yields track policy rates – akin to yields on cash-management instruments – whereas under the activity-based model, yields are much more volatile. By turning stablecoins into substitutes for bank deposits or money market funds or into funding instruments for exchanges' risky activities, remuneration models may shape the macro financial implications of wide stablecoin adoption in the future.

entries.title: New Book: The Stolguard Incident
entries.description:
My new sci fi thriller, The Stolguard Incident, is now available! It can be purchased in print, ebook, or audio versions on Amazon here. You can also find the print version on Barnes & Noble here, or you may be able to ask your local bookstore to order it for you. Premise: In the late […] 
 The post  New Book: The Stolguard Incident  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: Fractional Reserve Banking 101
entries.description:
Banks work in ways that are unintuitive for a lot of people. And yet, understanding the banking system as a whole is really helpful for investing, because having a grasp of how the banking system is functioning helps you determine whether you should expect monetary inflation or disinflation, and whether the risks of widespread financial […] 
 The post  Fractional Reserve Banking 101  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: Why Most Cryptocurrencies Won’t Accrue Value
entries.description:
December 2025 For many years now, I’ve been bullish on bitcoin, and continue to be, but have not extended that view toward any other cryptocurrency. That decision continues to hold up well. Since I first recommended it in April 2020, it has gone from $6,900 to $126,000, and is currently back down to around $85,000. […] 
 The post  Why Most Cryptocurrencies Won’t Accrue Value  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: The Rise of Bitcoin Stocks and Bonds
entries.description:
Originally published: July 2025 During the past year or so, the Bitcoin ecosystem has largely been led by the rise of corporate bitcoin treasury strategies. Although Strategy (MSTR) pioneered the trend back in 2020, the follow-on adoption by others was slow. However, after yet another cycle of bear market and bull market activity, as well […] 
 The post  The Rise of Bitcoin Stocks and Bonds  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: Twitter/X Restored
entries.description:
My Twitter/X account was compromised from February 26th to March 3rd, but I confirm that I’ve regained control of it now. Thank you to everyone who helped make it happen. Fortunately, I still had access to Nostr during that time (e.g. primal.net/lyn), which is decentralized social media. Eight months ago, at the Oslo Freedom Forum, […] 
 The post  Twitter/X Restored  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: Full Steam Ahead: All Aboard Fiscal Dominance
entries.description:
This research piece on fiscal dominance in the United States was compiled and written by Sam Callahan, and commissioned and advised by Lyn Alden. Published: January 2025 Executive Summary • Structural fiscal deficits have surpassed private sector lending and monetary policy as the primary drivers of economic activity and inflation, marking a fundamental shift in […] 
 The post  Full Steam Ahead: All Aboard Fiscal Dominance  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: Bitcoin: A Global Liquidity Barometer
entries.description:
This research piece on the correlation between measures of liquidity and the price of Bitcoin was compiled and written by Sam Callahan, and commissioned and advised by Lyn Alden. Published: September 2024 Executive Summary Bitcoin moves in the direction of global liquidity 83% of the time in any given 12-month period, which is higher than […] 
 The post  Bitcoin: A Global Liquidity Barometer  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: A New Look at Corporate Treasury Strategy
entries.description:
Published: August 2024 In the modern era of corporate finance, the balance sheet of a corporation has trended toward being less important relative to the income statement. Back during Benjamin Graham’s quintessential value investing days, he looked at balance sheet valuation metrics almost as much as he did income-related valuation metrics. But in modern times, […] 
 The post  A New Look at Corporate Treasury Strategy  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: The Power of Nostr: Decentralized Social Media and More
entries.description:
Published: July 2024 It would be powerful if you could bring your digital identity, content, and followers from one social ecosystem, such as Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, or Substack, to another. Imagine if you could publish content and send messages in any of those types of ecosystems, with one continuous digital identity rather than separate accounts. […] 
 The post  The Power of Nostr: Decentralized Social Media and More  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.title: Broken Money Explainer Video
entries.description:
In my book Broken Money, I wrote about the past, present, and future of money through the lens of technology. And importantly, I put a lot of focus on connecting some of those technological or quantitative elements to the human impact that they have had. I have now written, narrated, and produced a 30-minute explainer […] 
 The post  Broken Money Explainer Video  appeared first on  Lyn Alden .

entries.description: A top tier RL environment startup spawns out of thin air, the most aggressive compute ramp we've ever seen, 2000km+ scale-across, and some advice for Google DeepMind

entries.title: Anthropic 3Q26 Profit Over $1B: The Anthropic IPO Financials Sneak Peak
entries.description: Anthropic’s Opportunity is Theirs to Lose

entries.title: Nvidia GPU Debt Backstop Unleashes the AI Project Trinity: Capital, Offtake and Datacenters
entries.description: Over 7T AI debt by 2029, There can be no Neoclouds without the Trinity. Nvidia's Backstop Economics Explained. AI Debt Needs Quantified. Nvidia's Objective is to Broaden Compute Access, Develop AI Financing, Grow Neoclouds.

entries.title: Meta Compute: Everyone Wants To Be A Neocloud
entries.description: Zuck Takes Plan B? SpaceX 2.0, Bedrock 2.0, MSL Isn't Giving Up, Scaling RecSys by 10x... ClusterMAX ranking coming soon?

entries.title: EMIB-T Roadmap, Custom HBM, HBM4 Packaging Challenges, Microfluidic Cooling, Photonic Interconnects, and More
entries.description: ECTC 2026 Roundup, Intel, TSMC, SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron, Marvell, Lightmatter, Microsoft

entries.title: TokenBudgeting: Our Conversations with Enterprises on Token Spend
entries.description: Was Widespread TokenMaxxing Ever Really Here?

entries.title: US Grid Constraints: Towards 40GW+ of Behind-The-Meter Datacenter by 2028?
entries.description: Why the Grid Can't Keep Up, and Why that Drives Behind-The-Meter 50%+ of DCs/Year By 2028

entries.title: China’s CXMT Is Set to Challenge DRAM Incumbents
entries.description: CXMT IPO, SKHynix, Micron, Samsung Competition, Process Node Deficit, China HBM, Wafer Adds, Memory LTAs

entries.title: Stop Saying Half of 2026 US Datacenter Capacity Is Canceled
entries.description: Don't Believe The Vibecoded Estimates, Go Through Every Individual Filing Instead

entries.title: RL Systems Mind the Gap: Matching Trainer and Generator Throughput
entries.description: RL Training Infrastructure, GRPO, PipelineRL, Async RL, Policy Staleness, RL Sandbox Infra, CPU Requirements, TCO Analysis, Thinking Machines Tinker

feeds.name: Commodity Context
feeds.description: Data-centric, visualization-forward oil market research | Thematic deep-dives, monthly data reports, and a weekly market wrap-up every Friday

entries.title: Context Call: July 2026 (Video)
entries.description: Post-MOU status of shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz, the ongoing depression in China’s crude import appetite, and the emergence of the “mini-glut” currently being digested by oil markets.

entries.title: [FREE] Is Big Oil ‘Price Gouging’?
entries.description: Reduced global refinery capacity, not corporate greed, is keeping pump prices high.

entries.title: North American Oil Data Deck (July 2026)
entries.description: Continental petroleum production jumped back to near all-time highs in April on the back of the reacceleration of previously slowing upstream growth in both the US and Canada.

entries.title: REMINDER: Context Call Tomorrow
entries.description: Subscribe for details and registration link; paid subscribers will find link below paywall

entries.title: Oil Context Weekly (W27)
entries.description: Crude prices were effectively flat this week amidst a comparatively-quiet stretch of summer headlines, with all eyes on the optimistic-but-lurching resumption of Middle Eastern shipping activity.

entries.title: Getting Our Bearings Strait
entries.description: Assessing the pace and sustainability of the faster-than-expected post-MOU recovery in oil flows—both ways—through the Strait of Hormuz and the still-lagging pace of fresh Gulf loadings.

entries.title: Oil Context Weekly (W26)
entries.description: Crude fell another $8.50/bbl and Brent slipped into prompt contango for the first since February as the surge of exiting Hormuz barrels hit a still weak Asian import market with China on the sidelines

entries.title: Global Oil Data Deck (June 2026)
entries.description: Oil markets remained in deep supply deficit through April as the ongoing Middle Eastern production shut-ins were only partially offset by the pullback in Asian petrochemical feedstock demand.

entries.title: Oil & Iran War Context Weekly (W25)
entries.description: Crude plunged and curves flirted with prompt contango on the back of massive speculative short-selling following the announcement and signing of the US-Iran MOU to end the Iran War and reopen Hormuz.

entries.title: OPEC+ Data Deck (June 2026)
entries.description: Middle Eastern crude production inches higher as Gulf producers gained on incremental Hormuz Crisis logistical improvements; the Emiratis have left the building after 59 years as a member of the group

feeds.name: CSET — Georgetown AI Policy
feeds.channel: Center for Security and Emerging Technology
feeds.description: The place to find CSET's publications, reports, and people

entries.title: Anthropic Thinks Its Own Success Is Key to Making AI Safe
entries.description:
CSET’s Helen Toner shared her expert insight in an article published by WIRED. The article explores Anthropic’s philosophy of advancing cutting-edge AI while simultaneously positioning itself as a leader in AI safety. 
 The post  Anthropic Thinks Its Own Success Is Key to Making AI Safe  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: A major online safety bill for kids just passed the House. Here’s what experts say parents need to know
entries.description:
CSET’s Jessica Ji shared her expert insight in an article published by CNBC. The article examines the House passage of the Kids Internet and Digital Safety (KIDS) Act, a bill aimed at strengthening protections for minors online through age verification, content restrictions, and parental oversight tools. 
 The post  A major online safety bill for kids just passed the House. Here’s what experts say parents need to know  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: You Can Now Sound the Alarm on AI Behaving Badly
entries.description:
CSET’s Jessica Ji shared her expert insight in an article published by WIRED. The article examines the launch of FLARE-AI, a new crowdsourced platform designed to improve transparency and accountability by creating a centralized system for reporting harmful AI behavior and model flaws. 
 The post  You Can Now Sound the Alarm on AI Behaving Badly  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: When cheap AI becomes a secret weapon
entries.description:
CSET’s Sam Bresnick shared his expert insight in a newsletter segment published by Politico. The article examines the evolving U.S.–China AI competition, focusing on how cost-efficient Chinese AI models are beginning to gain traction globally and what that could mean for the long-term balance of technological and economic power in artificial intelligence. 
 The post  When cheap AI becomes a secret weapon  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: Trump administration’s AI crackdown opens door for China to close gap
entries.description:
CSET’s Sam Bresnick shared his expert insight in an article published by CNBC. The article examines U.S. AI restrictions and China’s rapid progress in closing the gap with leading American models. 
 The post  Trump administration’s AI crackdown opens door for China to close gap  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: The Long Arm of the Chinese Academy of Sciences
entries.description:
CSET’s Hanna Dohmen shared her expert insight in an article published by The Wire China. The article examines how the Chinese Academy of Sciences maintains long-term relationships with companies it supports, extending beyond initial investment or spinouts into ongoing collaboration across research, talent development, and shared resources.  
 The post  The Long Arm of the Chinese Academy of Sciences  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
entries.description:
CSET’s Hanna Dohmen shared her expert insight in an article published by The Verge. The piece examines the U.S. government’s unprecedented use of export controls to restrict access to Anthropic’s AI models. 
 The post  Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: Anthropic-U.S. battle highlights AI power struggle
entries.description:
CSET’s Helen Toner shared her expert insight in an article published by Axios. The article examines the U.S. government’s intervention involving Anthropic’s AI models and the broader debate over how frontier AI systems should be regulated and overseen. 
 The post  Anthropic-U.S. battle highlights AI power struggle  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: U.S. Semiconductors and China’s AI Military Ambitions
entries.description:
CSET’s Sam Bresnick shared his expert perspective in an op-ed published by Perry World House. In his piece, he argues that relaxing U.S. restrictions on advanced AI semiconductor exports to China would undermine long-term U.S. technological advantage. 
 The post  U.S. Semiconductors and China’s AI Military Ambitions  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .

entries.title: What Do AI Standards Mean for Small and Medium Enterprises?
entries.description:
While AI standards and best practices provide valuable guidance to practitioners, they often are geared toward integrating AI into the structure and practices of large, well-resourced organizations. Yet small and medium enterprises (SMEs) stand to benefit greatly from AI adoption as well. This blog examines the implications of AI standards for smaller organizations and proposes several achievable initial steps that practitioners can take to further responsible AI deployment under resource constraints. 
 The post  What Do AI Standards Mean for Small and Medium Enterprises?  appeared first on  Center for Security and Emerging Technology .
